sections.paragraph: (from the root אָבָה No. 2) poverty, misery, a word: once found, Pro, 23:29 , of the form קְמֹול, no doubt formed for the purpose of paronomastically answering to the words אֹוי and הֹוי; compare Lehrg. 374, note r, and Isa 15:4 17:1 59:13 so Abulwalid, whom I unhesitatingly follow. Kimchi, who is followed by most of the moderns, takes it as the same as אֹוי and הֹוי an interjection of grief. [“O, woe! Comp. Gr. αἰβοῖ, Arist. Pac. 1066.”-Ges. add.]
